name: Beirão (Beira Portuguese)
notes: Beirão / Beira Portuguese (Beira Litoral: Coimbra, Aveiro; Beira Alta: Viseu, Guarda; Beira Baixa: Castelo Branco), a northern-central EP dialect. Cintra places the Beirões in the Baixo-Minhoto-Duriense-Beirão group. MODELLED FEATURES: (1) APICO-ALVEOLAR-ONLY SIBILANT SYSTEM — the 'famous' s beirão: ⟨s/ss⟩ AND ⟨c/ç/z⟩ all realise as apico-alveolar [s̺]/[z̺] (Cintra 1971 pp.88, 93 — the most palatalised apical variant is 'vulgarmente conhecida pelo nome de s beirão'; Álvarez Pérez 2014 pp.37-38, Beirões 'kept only the apico-alveolar branch'; Pérez notes the apical core is clearest in the districts of Viseu, Guarda and Castelo Branco). Coda ⟨s/z⟩ keep chiado [ʃ]/[ʒ]. (2) BETACISM — /v/–/b/ merged to /b/ ~ [β]; Pérez 2014 pp.35-37 documents an extensive bilabial-merger area in the eastern districts of Guarda and Castelo Branco that Cintra's original map omitted. A conservative alveolar rhotic (trill [r]) is enumerated as a variant of /ʁ/. (3) BEIRA-BAIXA STRESSED /u/ → [y] PALATALISATION — Cintra (1971, p.14 of the CVC reflow) delimits the Beira-Baixa / Alto-Alentejo zone — 'uma região que tem como principais núcleos urbanos Castelo-Branco e Portalegre' — by exactly the isogloss of stressed-/u/ palatalisation: 'Como isófona que possa marcar o limite da zona, parece-me preferível escolher, a da palatalização, em maior ou menor grau, da vogal tónica u', within 'uma profunda alteração de timbre de todo o sistema vocálico, principalmente do tónico'; he adds that at the zone's edges the u-timbre change is the trait that stays most perceptible ('o traço que permanece mais perceptível é a alteração do timbre do u'). The explicit front-rounded [y] IPA value is written in the adjacent Barlavento-Algarvio passage of the same 'reacção em cadeia' — 'a palatalização da lábio-velar [u] em [y]' — the two zones sharing the /u/-palatalisation isogloss. Encoded as the allophone rule BEI_U_FRONTING (stressed nucleus only), mirroring the sister mainland zone pt-PT-x-alentejo (ALE_U_FRONTING) and pt-PT-x-algarve: because this is a whole-tonic-system MAINLAND chain shift (not the insular São Miguel open-nucleus process), it fronts even before a tautosyllabic coda liquid (sul → [ˈs̺yɫ]) — deliberately WITHOUT the pt-PT-x-acores ACO_U_KEEP_BEFORE_CODA block, which is a Rogers-1948 São-Miguel-specific conditioning with no mainland source. A PROCLITIC GUARD (word_exceptions, same design as pt-PT-x-alentejo/pt-PT-x-algarve) pins the monosyllabic clitics o/os/no/nos/do/dos/ao/aos/pelo/pelos/um/uns to their inherited Beira nuclei so the word-level stress detector cannot mis-front do → [ˈdy] etc. SCOPE / INTRA-NODE LIMIT (documented, not faked): /u/-fronting is the Beira-BAIXA (Castelo-Branco) sub-zone realisation; Beira Litoral (Coimbra, Aveiro) and Beira Alta (Viseu, Guarda) sit in the Baixo-Minhoto-Duriense-Beirão group and do NOT palatalise tonic /u/ — this general Beira node models the strongest (Beira-Baixa) realisation, the same modelling stance as pt-PT-x-acores (Terceira gold vs the São Miguel /u/-fronting). The wider chain shift ([a] velarisation, [e]/[ɛ] labialisation, [ɔ]/[o] raising) is left to notes to avoid collapsing the vowel space; only the categorical /u/ → [y] is realised. Inherits pt-PT base. ACOUSTIC REFINEMENT (Brissos 2014b, 'A vogal u', AVOC): the /u/-fronting is context-conditioned — stressed /u/ is NOT fronted after a bilabial onset (/p,b,m/), where it retracts toward [u] (Centro-Interior/Alpalhão: 11% of the F2 space after bilabial, 'identificando-se assim com [u]'). Encoded as the guard rule BEI_U_NO_FRONT_AFTER_BILABIAL, ordered before BEI_U_FRONTING. Brissos 2014b also notes the CI /u/ is acoustically central ([ʉ]~[y]); the [y] value kept here follows Cintra and the narrow example transcriptions of Brissos (2014, JPL) ([lˈymɨ] lume) and is documented, not overturned.

sources.author: Vasconcelos, J.L. de
sources.title: Esquisse d'une dialectologie portugaise
sources.notes: Classical dialectological survey; Beira section foundational

sources.author: Cintra, L. F. Lindley
sources.title: Nova proposta de classificação dos dialectos galego-portugueses
sources.notes: Foundational EP dialect classification. p.93: the Baixo-Minhoto-Duriense-Beirao group reduces the archaic four-sibilant system to two apico-alveolar sibilants [s̺ z̺]; p.88: /v/-/b/ merger and apico-alveolar sibilants are diagnostic northern features. p.14 (CVC reflow): delimits the Beira-Baixa / Alto-Alentejo zone (núcleos Castelo-Branco e Portalegre) by the isogloss of stressed-/u/ palatalisation — 'Como isófona que possa marcar o limite da zona, parece-me preferível escolher, a da palatalização, em maior ou menor grau, da vogal tónica u' — within 'uma profunda alteração de timbre de todo o sistema vocálico, principalmente do tónico', noting the u-timbre change is the trait most perceptible at the zone's edges; the explicit [y] value ('a palatalização da lábio-velar [u] em [y]') is written in the adjacent Barlavento-Algarvio passage of the same 'reacção em cadeia'. Grounds BEI_U_FRONTING for the Beira-Baixa (Castelo Branco) sub-zone.

sources.author: Álvarez Pérez, Xosé Afonso
sources.title: European Portuguese dialectal features: a comparison with Cintra's proposal
sources.notes: ALEPG-corpus quantitative reassessment. pp.37-38: Baixo-Minhotos, Durienses and Beirões 'kept only the apico-alveolar branch' (both s/ss and c/ç/z merge to apical), vs the conservative four-sibilant Transmontano/Alto-Minhoto. pp.35-37: /b/-/v/ merger extends along the coast beyond Coimbra and through the eastern districts (Guarda, Castelo Branco).

sources.author: Brissos, Fernando
sources.title: New insights into Portuguese central-southern dialects: understanding their present and past forms through acoustic data from stressed vowels
sources.notes: Acoustic study of central-southern EP stressed vowels (the zone Cintra delimits by the stressed-/u/ isogloss, spanning Beira-Baixa to the Algarve). Documents stressed /u/ → [y] ([tˈyd] 'tudo', [lˈymɨ] 'lume') and cites Lüdtke (1957) and Maia (1975–78) that the palatalisation is stressed-only — corroborating acoustic source for the Beira-Baixa BEI_U_FRONTING modelled from Cintra's p.14 zone-defining isogloss.

sources.author: Brissos, Fernando
sources.title: A vogal u, os dialectos do Centro-Sul português e a dialectologia acústica
sources.publisher: Textos Selecionados, XXIX Encontro Nacional da Associação Portuguesa de Linguística, Porto: APL, pp. 85-102
sources.notes: Acoustic-atlas (AVOC) study of stressed /u/ across 13 Centro-Sul survey points. Refinement over Brissos (2014, JPL): the /u/-fronting is CONTEXT-CONDITIONED — after a bilabial onset (/p,b,m/) the vowel loses most of its advancement and sits near [u] (Praia da Salema / Barlavento: after bilabial u = 25% of the F2 acoustic space, 'muito mais perto de [u] do que de [y]', vs 64% after alveo-dental/palatal/velar, pp.87-88; Alpalhão / Centro-Interior: after bilabial u = 11%, 'identificando-se assim com [u]', p.89). Grounds the bilabial guard (stressed /u/ kept [u] after /p,b,m/). Also documents that the fronting spans [ʉ]~[y] (acoustically central, most often written [ʉ] for the Centro-Interior, [y]/threshold-of-[y] for the Barlavento, p.87) and that across the Sul /u/ is frequently NOT the most-backed vowel (7 of 13 points, pp.91-93) — kept to notes, not modelled.

stress.notes: Acordo Ortográfico 1990 conventions: paroxytone default; written accents override; final -i/-u/-r/-l/-z/-x/-im/-om/-um/-ns and nasal diphthongs attract final stress. Unmarked -em/-am stay paroxytone (homem, falam). DIPHTHONGS: declared so the bundled syllabifier counts nuclei correctly — without them every vowel run merges into one nucleus and co-e-lho is read as coe-lho, one syllable short, putting the stress and its vowel reduction on the wrong vowel. ⟨oe⟩ is hiatus, not a diphthong (Cunha & Cintra, §Ditongos).

allophone_rules.notes: Bilabial-context guard: the stressed labio-velar /u/ is NOT fronted after a bilabial onset (/p,b,m/). Brissos (2014b, 'A vogal u', AVOC acoustic atlas) shows the fronting is context-conditioned — after a bilabial the vowel loses most of its advancement and identifies with [u] (Praia da Salema/Barlavento: 25% of the F2 space after bilabial vs 64% elsewhere, pp.87-88; Alpalhão/Centro-Interior: 11%, 'identificando-se assim com [u]', p.89). Ordered BEFORE the fronting rule so it wins (first-matching rule fires): after /p,b,m/ the guard keeps [u]; in every other onset context the fronting rule downstream applies. Categorical simplification of a gradient effect (after a bilabial the vowel is 'much closer to [u]', not exactly [u]); the gradience is documented here, not encoded.

Palatalisation/fronting of the stressed labio-velar /u/ to the front rounded [y] — the feature by which Cintra (1971, p.14 of the CVC reflow) delimits the Beira-Baixa / Alto-Alentejo zone (núcleos Castelo-Branco e Portalegre): 'a palatalização, em maior ou menor grau, da vogal tónica u', part of 'uma profunda alteração de timbre de todo o sistema vocálico, principalmente do tónico'. The explicit [y] value ('a palatalização da lábio-velar [u] em [y]') is written in the adjacent Barlavento-Algarvio passage of the same 'reacção em cadeia'; Brissos (2014) documents it acoustically for the central-southern zone ([tˈyd] tudo, [lˈymɨ] lume). Restricted to the STRESSED nucleus (stress='stressed'): unstressed /u/ is untouched, so it does not feed on the inherited pt-PT unstressed /o/→[u] reduction. Being a whole-tonic-system MAINLAND chain shift, it fronts even before a tautosyllabic coda liquid (sul → [ˈs̺yɫ]), mirroring pt-PT-x-alentejo/pt-PT-x-algarve and deliberately WITHOUT the insular pt-PT-x-acores ACO_U_KEEP_BEFORE_CODA block (a Rogers-1948 São-Miguel open-nucleus conditioning with no mainland source). NOTES-ONLY (not modelled): the wider chain shift ([a] velarisation, [e]/[ɛ] labialisation, [ɔ]/[o] raising) is left to notes to avoid collapsing the vowel space. SCOPE: the Beira-BAIXA (Castelo-Branco) sub-zone realisation — Beira Litoral/Alta do not palatalise tonic /u/; this general Beira node models the strongest realisation (same stance as pt-PT-x-acores). A proclitic guard (see word_exceptions) keeps monosyllabic clitics — o/os/no/nos/do/dos/ao/aos/pelo/pelos/um/uns — on their inherited [u] nucleus, since the word-level stress detector mis-marks them as stressed.

location.notes: Viseu — a representative locality of the Beiras (Beira Litoral, Alta and Baixa), an area far larger than any one town. [Wikidata Q117676 P625]
